name: shepi
description: AI-assisted Quality of Earnings analysis for M&A due diligence. Connect ChatGPT or Claude to your shepi projects to list deals, review adjustments, and generate QoE summaries.

servers.name: shepi-chatgpt
servers.description: MCP server for ChatGPT with interactive widget UI. Renders project summaries and QoE metrics as inline components.

servers.name: shepi-mcp
servers.description: MCP server for Claude and other general MCP clients.

tools.name: echo
tools.description: Smoke test — echoes the input text.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List the signed-in user's shepi projects.

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Create a new shepi QoE project.

tools.name: get_project_summary
tools.description: Get a project summary card (renders as widget in ChatGPT).

tools.name: get_quality_of_earnings_summary
tools.description: Get computed QoE metrics for a project (renders as widget in ChatGPT).

tools.name: list_documents
tools.description: List documents uploaded to a project.

tools.name: list_adjustments
tools.description: List pending or reviewed adjustments on a project.

tools.name: update_adjustment_status
tools.description: Approve, reject, or mark an adjustment as reviewed.

tools.name: get_adjustment_detail
tools.description: Get full detail for one adjustment (Claude only).

tools.name: get_export_data
tools.description: Return structured project data plus a download URL for the QoE workbook.

tools.name: run_discovery
tools.description: Start AI adjustment discovery for a project (async; poll get_discovery_status).

tools.name: get_discovery_status
tools.description: Check discovery job status and how many adjustments have been found.

tools.name: validate_financial_statement
tools.description: Validate an uploaded financial statement against the project's trial balance.

resources.name: llms.txt
resources.description: Concise content manifest with quotable definitions and example queries.

resources.name: llms-full.txt
resources.description: Extended manifest with full FAQ and resource catalog.

notes: Both MCP endpoints share the same Supabase OAuth 2.1 authorization server with PKCE and dynamic client registration — no manual client_id/secret provisioning needed. Data access is scoped to the authenticated user via Postgres RLS.
